title: Author a least-privilege IAM policy document
description: Produce a JSON policy scoped to exact actions, resource ARNs, and condition keys, then validate it with the policy simulator.
body: Use when you must grant an AWS principal exactly the access it needs. Do not start from an AWS managed policy with broad scope and trim later. Actions: list the precise API calls the code makes, build a Statement per logical access with explicit Action and Resource ARNs, add condition keys to bind it to the calling service or tag, and run iam simulate-principal-policy against both intended and forbidden actions. Validation signals include the simulator returning Allow only for listed actions and Deny for everything else, plus a clean CloudTrail on the real call. Risk boundary: if the task seems to need a wildcard or a cross-account trust, stop and escalate for human approval.
